raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
January 2026 closed with total actuals of £8,546 against a budget of £580, producing a net favourable variance of £7,966 (+1,373.4%). The overrun is concentrated almost entirely in the Sales line, which accounts for £5,121 of the significant-line variance and is the sole item requiring narrative explanation. The driver is structural and well-evidenced: three deals originally forecast to close in Q4 2025 (Redwood Group, Apex Analytics, and Fortis Ltd) slipped past the 31 December cut-off and were signed in early January, with revenue recognition formally approved by the CFO. The January Sales budget of £500 was set on the assumption of a quiet post-Christmas month, so it was never designed to absorb this pipeline carry-over. The variance is favourable, approved, and understood. No adverse items are present across the remainder of the report; all other line variances are immaterial.

LINE COMMENTARY

Sales | +£5,121 (+1024.1%) | Budget: £500 | Actual: £5,621
Actuals exceeded budget by £5,121, representing a +1,024.1% favourable variance. The variance is driven by the late signing of three Q4 2025 deals that had been expected to close before 31 December 2025 but carried over into January.
Key facts from the evidence:
- Redwood Group, Apex Analytics, and Fortis Ltd all signed during 8–9 January 2026, confirmed as Closed Won in CRM.
- CRM records the combined ARR of the three deals at £5,250 (Redwood: £2,100; Apex: £1,850; Fortis: £1,300). First-month combined billing is noted in Slack at approximately £3,200, which likely reflects the initial invoiced portion rather than full ARR. The precise amount recognised against the £5,621 actual may include additional billing components and requires confirmation against the underlying invoices.
- A CEO memo issued 8 January formally notified the CFO of the Q4 carry-over, and revenue recognition for all three deals was approved by the CFO.
- A CEO/CFO pipeline review was calendared for 7 January, confirming awareness of the likely January close ahead of signing.
- The January budget of £500 was set assuming a quiet month, with no allowance for pipeline carry-over; the variance was therefore expected and approved by leadership.
The variance is favourable, structurally explained by timing, and has formal CFO approval. No remedial action is required, though finance may wish to note that future budgets should reflect pipeline carry-over risk to avoid similarly large positive distortions.
Sources
- Slack - 2026-01-08T00:00:00Z - slack-sales-broad - "Big news — three Q4 deals that didn't close before Christmas have landed in January. Redwood, Apex, and Fortis all signed this week. Combined first-month billing £3,200. The January budget was set at £500 assuming a quiet month — looks like the pipeline catch-up exceeded expectations."
- Gmail - 2026-01-08T00:00:00Z - gmail-sales-broad - "Q4 pipeline carry-over memo from CEO — three major deals (Redwood, Apex, Fortis) that were forecast to close by 31 December 2025 have now signed in January. Revenue recognition approved by CFO. January budget variance is expected and approved."
- CRM - 2026-01-09T00:00:00Z - crm-sales-broad - "CRM pipeline report January 2026. Redwood Group — Closed Won 8 Jan (ARR £2,100). Apex Analytics — Closed Won 8 Jan (ARR £1,850). Fortis Ltd — Closed Won 9 Jan (ARR £1,300). Total carry-over from Q4 2025: £5,250."
- Calendar - 2026-01-07T00:00:00Z - calendar-sales-broad - "Pipeline review — CEO and CFO 1:1 to discuss Q4 deals that missed December close. Three deals confirmed for January signing."

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following line items were assessed and deemed immaterial. No detailed investigation was performed.
- Audit & Accountancy Fees | Variance: £47
- Bank Fees | Variance: £15
- Entertainment — 100% Business | Variance: £13
- General Expenses | Variance: £308
- Light Power Heating | Variance: £49 (+61.7%)
- Motor Vehicle Expenses | Variance: £124
- Printing & Stationery | Variance: £18
- Rent | Variance: £984
- Subscriptions | Variance: £1,220
- Telephone & Internet | Variance: £37
- Travel National | Variance: £29
